data.title: Implement generative recipe rollback in recipe-executor.sh (track created files, delete on failure)
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## Description
Update plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-executor.sh (created in story 5108-39a1) to handle the generative rollback path when recipe_type=generative.

### Changes to recipe-executor.sh:
1. After loading the registry entry, read recipe_type field (default: transform when absent).
2. For recipe_type=transform: existing git stash path (unchanged).
3. For recipe_type=generative: Before adapter invocation, snapshot directory contents (find . -name '*' -newer /tmp/recipe-snapshot-marker). After adapter failure, delete all files that are new since the snapshot (files created during the failed execution).
4. Track snapshot file: `touch /tmp/recipe-snapshot-marker-$$` before adapter invocation, `find . -newer /tmp/recipe-snapshot-marker-$$ -type f` to get created files, `rm -f` each created file on failure.
5. Clean up snapshot marker on exit.
6. On success: no cleanup (generative recipes intentionally leave created files).

### Alternative: pass --recipe-type to adapter
The adapter receives RECIPE_TYPE env var. The executor still owns the rollback decision based on RECIPE_TYPE, but the adapter can also report created files via files_changed in the output JSON (which executor uses for targeted cleanup).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] recipe-executor.sh handles recipe_type=generative
  Verify: grep -q 'recipe_type\|RECIPE_TYPE\|generative' plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-executor.sh
- [ ] test-recipe-executor.sh passes including generative rollback test (from T-SR-03)
  Verify: bash tests/scripts/test-recipe-executor.sh
- [ ] bash -n syntax check passes
  Verify: bash -n plugins/dso/scripts/recipe-executor.sh
